response.justification: The candidate has a long and varied software development career but is fundamentally a web and full-stack developer whose primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and JavaScript. C++ is conspicuously absent from the skills section; the only relevant C++ exposure is a 6-month stint at National Instruments writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor's thesis project. The role demands 8–12 years of deep C++ and STL proficiency, multi-threaded systems design, industrial communication protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 104), and adherence to IEC 62443-4-1 secure development standards — none of which appear meaningfully in this profile.
strengths: Node.js and React experience directly matches the listed advantageous skills for the role
Demonstrated ability to build high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby — millions of HTTP requests per second)
Event-driven and message-broker architecture experience through the GISCollective open-source platform
concerns: C++ is not listed in the candidate's skills section; documented C++ use is limited to a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis
Zero evidence of industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0), or IEC 62443 / SDL security practices
Entire post-2015 career trajectory is web and cloud-native development, with no systems or embedded programming progression

key_factors.factor: No industrial protocols or IEC 62443 experience
